One sunny afternoon, Cora the Crane Truck polished her silver hook at Lantern Yard. She was ready for a careful delivery.
Pippa arrived with a new wooden sign for the Pinecone Reading Nook. An open-book picture gleamed on its front.
A playful hilltop breeze fluttered the marigolds. When Cora tested the lift, the wide sign grew wobbly.
Cora lowered the sign at once. “A safe load should feel calm and steady,” she said.
Cora shortened her boom and turned her truck toward the breeze. The next tiny lift wobbled much less.
Tuck rolled over with two soft guide ropes on his hitch. “These can help the sign stay straight,” he said.
Pippa clipped one guide rope to each lower corner. Tuck held both ropes gently with his sturdy hitch.
Cora lifted again, slow and smooth. The sign stayed straight while Tuck rolled one small step beside it.
Together they left Lantern Yard along the smooth pine path. Cora carried the steady sign low and close.
At the first bend, the same breeze slipped between the pine trees. Cora stopped before the sign could sway.
A thick hedge made a quiet windbreak beside the path. The friends waited there until the pine needles grew still.
Soon the lanterns of the reading nook twinkled ahead. Cora carried the sign through the last quiet turn.
Cora lowered the sign into its prepared base. Tuck kept the guide ropes steady until it stood safely on its own.
The new sign stood straight beside the cozy books. “Slow, low, and steady worked,” Cora said with a proud smile.
That night, Cora folded her boom beneath the quiet moon. She closed her sleepy hazel eyes and dreamed of steady, gentle lifts.
